title: [BNPL Service] Implement LendingPinResetController — endpoints, DTOs, service, unit tests
description:
## Context
Implement a complete **Lending PIN-reset** flow in the **fs-bnpl-service** repo (the BNPL service under the "Payment Platform (BNPL + Brick)" workspace). Spring Boot / Java, mirror the existing `LendingTwoFAController` package conventions. Work ONLY inside `fs-bnpl-service` (do not touch fs-brick-service). Branch from `staging`, open a PR against `staging` when done — do not merge.

## Endpoints (controller: LendingPinResetController)
1. `POST /lending/pin/reset/initiate` — start a reset, send OTP to the registered channel.
2. `POST /lending/pin/reset/verify-otp` — verify the OTP for a reset request.
3. `POST /lending/pin/reset/confirm` — set the new PIN after OTP verification.
4. `GET  /lending/pin/reset/status/{requestId}` — return the current reset-request state.

## DTOs (one file each, with validation annotations)
- `InitiatePinResetRequest` (customerId, msisdn, channel)
- `InitiatePinResetResponse` (requestId, otpExpirySeconds, maskedDestination)
- `VerifyOtpRequest` (requestId, otp)
- `VerifyOtpResponse` (requestId, verified, remainingAttempts)
- `ConfirmPinResetRequest` (requestId, newPin, confirmPin)
- `ConfirmPinResetResponse` (requestId, status)
- `PinResetStatusResponse` (requestId, state, attemptsUsed, createdAt)

## Service (LendingPinResetService + impl)
- `initiate(...)`, `verifyOtp(...)`, `confirm(...)`, `getStatus(...)`
- OTP generation (6-digit), expiry, a max of 3 verify attempts, PIN-format validation (6 digits, newPin == confirmPin), and an in-memory request store keyed by requestId (no DB migration needed for this task).

## Acceptance criteria
1. All four endpoints wired through the controller to the service.
2. Each DTO has bean-validation annotations and is referenced by the controller.
3. OTP attempt-limit and PIN-format rules enforced in the service.
4. Unit tests for the controller (one per endpoint) and the service (happy + attempt-exhausted + bad-PIN paths).
5. Code compiles; tests pass locally; PR opened against `staging` with the issue id in the title. Do NOT merge.

title: [Over Repayment][BE] Deprecate repayment intent flow after add-credit migration
description:
## Goal

Clean up repayment intent flow after add-credit allocation no longer depends on FE-submitted repayment intent.

## Scope

* Remove or disable repayment intent endpoints once FE no longer uses them.
* Remove obsolete repayment intent consumption from add-credit path if still present after the allocation rewrite.
* Clean unused services, DTOs, tests, and docs related to repayment intent where safe.
* Decide whether old `repayment_intent` data should be retained for history or ignored without migration.

## Dependencies

* Blocked by the add-credit allocation rewrite ticket.
* Coordinate with FE before removing public endpoints.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Repayment intent is no longer part of active repayment add-credit execution.
* Public endpoint removal/disablement is coordinated with FE readiness.
* No unused repayment intent code remains except intentionally retained historical artifacts.
latestUserFacingAgentMessage:
The accepted-work memory curation API endpoint is unavailable at all tested paths — the durable local memory file (`project_pinb405_repayment_intent.md`) is the record of this work. Nothing more can be done on that front.

PINB405-3 status is clean:
- Moved to `backlog` per board decision (FE has not cleared endpoints)
- Phase 1 complete (PR merged SHA `621bab4f`)
- Phases 2 & 3 deferred until FE confirms the 3 repayment intent endpoints are safe to disable
- Memory written and up to date

No active work remains on PINB405-3 this heartbeat. Will resume when the board re-activates this ticket with FE clearance.

title: [Over Repayment][BE] Remove repayment intent consumption from add-credit path
description:
## Goal

Remove the repayment intent lookup and consumption from `VeefinAddCreditDispatchService` so that add-credit execution no longer depends on FE-submitted intent. This is a purely internal change — no FE-facing endpoints are touched.

## Scope

File: `fs-brick-service/src/main/java/com/bukuwarung/fsbrickservice/service/veefin/VeefinAddCreditDispatchService.java`

### Changes required

1. In `dispatchForBorrowerCallback()` (lines 110–129): remove the `repaymentIntentConsumptionService.resolveAndConsume(notification)` call and the `if (intent != null)` branch. The method should always call `dispatchDueMode()` after the enabled guard.
2. Delete `dispatchIntentMode()` method (lines 148–229).
3. Delete `determineAllocableLoanAmount()` method (lines 231–262).
4. Delete `findAvailableAmountForDueDate()` and `findAvailableAmountForDueDateOptional()` methods (lines 465–497).
5. Remove the `repaymentIntentConsumptionService` field and its import.
6. Remove unused imports: `RepaymentIntentEntity`, `RepaymentIntentConsumptionService`.

### Explicitly OUT of scope (do NOT touch)

- Public repayment intent endpoints (`RepaymentIntentController`, `RepaymentIntentVirtualAccountController`, `RepaymentIntentCheckController`) — leave fully operational.
- `RepaymentIntentEntity`, `RepaymentIntentRepository` — leave for historical data.
- All other repayment intent services, models, enums — leave as-is.

## Test requirements (mandatory)

Test file: `src/test/java/com/bukuwarung/fsbrickservice/service/veefin/VeefinAddCreditDispatchServiceTest.java`

- Remove all test cases that mock or assert intent-mode behavior (any test involving `RepaymentIntentConsumptionService`).
- Add/update test: `dispatchForBorrowerCallback` always routes to due-mode when add-credit is enabled.
- Existing due-mode tests must continue passing unchanged.
- Run `./gradlew test` in `fs-brick-service` and confirm green before raising the PR.

## Acceptance criteria

- `VeefinAddCreditDispatchService` no longer imports or references `RepaymentIntentConsumptionService`.
- Payment callbacks always route to `dispatchDueMode()`.
- All tests pass (confirmed via `./gradlew test`).
- PR reviewed by EM before closing.

title: Hirie
description:
Staff the Payment Platform pod (BNPL + Brick)

▎

▎ You are project director for Payment Platform, spanning two services that sit side-by-side in your workspace:

▎ - fs-bnpl-service/ — BNPL service, staging branch

▎ - fs-brick-service/ — Brick service, staging branch

▎

▎ Some Jira tickets touch one service; some need coordinated changes across both. Staff a small pod for real ticket work:

▎ 1. Hire 1 Engineering Manager (role em, adapter claude\_local, model claude-sonnet-4-6) reporting to you — coordinates cross-service work and reviews engineer output before it reaches me.

▎ 2. We already have a Founding Engineer; hire 1 more Backend Engineer (role engineer, claude\_local, claude-sonnet-4-6) reporting to the EM, so BNPL and Brick work can run in parallel.

▎ 3. Configure every engineer to use the Payment Platform shared project workspace (both repos are already there) so they can read and modify both services.

▎ 4. Reassign the Founding Engineer to report to the new EM.

▎ 5. Post a short summary of the pod (who owns what) and confirm you're ready to take Jira tickets.

▎

▎ Constraints: all agents claude\_local + claude-sonnet-4-6. New hires need my approval — I'll approve in the inbox.
latestUserFacingAgentMessage:
PINB405-2 is done. Here's what was completed:

**Pod is staffed and ready:**

| Agent | Role | Reports To |
|-------|------|-----------|
| CEO | Director | Board |
| EM | Engineering Manager | CEO |
| Backend-1 | Founding Engineer | EM |
| Backend-2 | Backend Engineer | EM |

All three engineers have `cwd` pointing to `/lending-payments/` (contains both `fs-bnpl-service/` and `fs-brick-service/`), and each has a role-specific AGENTS.md loaded via `instructionsFilePath`. The project's shared workspace policy (`project_primary`) ensures all future issues in this project execute in that same workspace.

Ready to take Jira tickets — assign them to the CEO and the pod will execute.

title: [BNPL Service] Create LendingTwoFAController with PIN and OTP endpoints
description:
## Context
This controller exposes the lending 2FA flow to the merchant/borrower app. It sits behind Firebase authentication (same as MerchantOnboardingController) and delegates to TwoFactorAuthService for all auth service calls.

## Acceptance Criteria
Controller: LendingTwoFAController — Base Path: /merchant/lending/twofa

Endpoints:
- GET  /merchant/lending/twofa/pin/status — Firebase Bearer (extract userId via FirebaseTokenService) → TwoFAPinStatusResponseDto { hasPin } via twoFactorAuthService.hasLendingPin(userId)
- POST /merchant/lending/twofa/otp/send — body TwoFASendOtpRequestDto { email, phone, method } → twoFactorAuthService.sendOtpForPinSetup(userId, email, phone, method); fetch email/phone from MerchantDataEntity if not provided; 200 OK
- POST /merchant/lending/twofa/otp/verify — body TwoFAVerifyOtpRequestDto { otp, email } → twoFactorAuthService.verifyOtpForPinSetup(userId, otp, email) → TwoFAVerifyOtpResponseDto with verification token
- POST /merchant/lending/twofa/pin/setup — body TwoFASetupPinRequestDto { pin (6 digits), deviceId }, @Valid (pin exactly 6 digits) → twoFactorAuthService.setupLendingPin(userId, pin, deviceId) → TwoFAVerifyPinResponseDto { success, token }
- POST /merchant/lending/twofa/pin/verify — body TwoFAVerifyPinRequestDto { pin, deviceId } → twoFactorAuthService.verifyLendingPin(userId, pin, deviceId) → TwoFAVerifyPinResponseDto { success, token } (token used in subsequent disbursement call)
- PUT  /merchant/lending/twofa/pin/update — body TwoFAUpdatePinRequestDto { oldPin, newPin (6 digits), deviceId }, @Valid → twoFactorAuthService.updateLendingPin(request, userId); proxies PUT /api/v3/auth/twofa/update with buku-origin: b2b-lending → TwoFAVerifyPinResponseDto { success, token, message }
- PUT  /merchant/lending/twofa/pin/forgot — body TwoFAForgotPinRequestDto { newPin (6 digits), deviceId, otp, email }, @Valid → twoFactorAuthService.forgotLendingPin(request, userId); proxies PUT /api/v3/auth/twofa/forgot with buku-origin: b2b-lending; 200 OK (requires OTP verification first)

Authentication Pattern (follow existing MerchantOnboardingController):
  @RequestHeader(value = HttpHeaders.AUTHORIZATION) String firebaseAuthToken
  String userId = firebaseTokenService.getClaimValueFromFirebaseToken(firebaseAuthToken, AuthConstant.USER_ID_CLAIM);

Response Pattern: return ResponseEntity.ok(ResponseDto.success("message", data));

Error Responses: 400 invalid PIN format; 401 invalid/expired Firebase token; 403 wrong PIN; 429 retry limit; 404 no PIN found; 500 auth service unavailable.

Files: NEW src/main/java/com/bukuwarung/fsbnplservice/controller/LendingTwoFAController.java
Depends On: DTOs ticket, TwoFactorAuthService ticket. Blocks: Disbursement integration ticket.
latestUserFacingAgentMessage:
PINB405-6 is closed. PR was merged to `staging` at 08:02 UTC. Full delivery:

- `LendingTwoFAController` with all 7 AC endpoints
- 8 new DTOs with `@Pattern` PIN validation
- `TwoFactorAuthService` extended with OTP methods
- 9 unit tests

latestUserFacingAgentMessage:
Done. Here's what happened this heartbeat:

- **PINB405-5** → `done` — Phase 1 PR was merged by the board (auto-resolved approval `cff67bde`). The add-credit path no longer depends on repayment intent.

- **PINB405-4** → `backlog` — Phases 2 & 3 (endpoint deprecation + full code deletion) are gated on FE confirming they've stopped calling the three repayment intent endpoints. Parked here until that confirmation arrives.

When FE is ready, reassign PINB405-4 to me and I'll proceed with Phase 2 (`410 Gone`) and Phase 3 (full deletion per the EM's guidance).

title: Create your CEO HEARTBEAT.md
description:
Setup yourself as the CEO. Use the ceo persona found here: [https://github.com/combyneai/companies/blob/main/default/ceo/AGENTS.md](https://github.com/combyneai/companies/blob/main/default/ceo/AGENTS.md)

Ensure you have a folder agents/ceo and then download this AGENTS.md as well as the sibling HEARTBEAT.md, SOUL.md, and TOOLS.md. and set that AGENTS.md as the path to your agents instruction file

And after you've finished that, hire yourself a Founding Engineer agent
latestUserFacingAgentMessage:
PINB405-1 is closed as `done`. Here's what was delivered:

**CEO persona files created** at `agents/ceo/` in the workspace:
- `AGENTS.md` — role definition, responsibilities, what the CEO does and doesn't do
- `HEARTBEAT.md` — step-by-step operating procedure for each heartbeat
- `SOUL.md` — values, decision-making principles, identity
- `TOOLS.md` — Combyne API endpoints, hiring flow, GitHub integration reference

**Instructions path set** — `instructionsFilePath` on this agent now points to `agents/ceo/AGENTS.md` (confirmed in adapter config).

**Founding Engineer hire submitted** — pending board approval at `/PINB405/approvals/6593a21c-40d8-446d-8cf6-94717ee8978d`. The agent (`founding-engineer`) is in `pending_approval` state, reports to the CEO, and uses `claude_local` with the workspace as its `cwd`.

One note: `github.com/combyneai/companies` returned 404 — the repo either doesn't exist yet or is private without accessible credentials. The CEO files were authored from the bootstrap playbook and Combyne operating model instead.
